content.jobHook: Build secure, scalable .NET and Python backends on Azure for public-sector safety projects, collaborating across teams to deliver quality results.
content.unEditedContent:
Seers Digital is not just another IT consulting company. We’re one of Australia’s fastest-growing tech firms, partnering with top-tier clients including state and federal government agencies, leading banks, and major utilities. Our mission is to deliver exceptional solutions that truly make an impact and to have fun doing it.


Why You’ll Love Working at Seers Digital:

- Exciting Projects: Work on purposeful projects with high-profile clients across government, finance, and utilities.

- Career Growth: Ongoing professional development, training, and opportunities to expand your skills.

- Empowered Culture: A dynamic, inclusive and supportive environment where you’re encouraged to own your work, innovate, and excel.

- Great People: Collaborate with talented, like-minded professionals who are passionate about delivering outstanding results.


What’s in it for You:

- Competitive salary and benefits.

- Flexible, hybrid work arrangements.

- Exposure to diverse industries and cutting-edge technology.

- A workplace that values initiative, creativity, and excellence.


Summary:

The Backend Developer will play a key role in delivering project-based solutions as part of a collaborative team. This position involves designing, developing, and maintaining secure, scalable backend systems using .NET and Python, with strong integration into Microsoft Azure services. The role supports public sector safety and justice initiatives, ensuring compliance with government standards and contributing to case management and business process applications. Success in this role requires working closely with cross-functional teams to meet project milestones and deliver high-quality outcomes.


Key Responsibilities:

- Application Development

  • Design and build robust backend solutions using .NET Core and Python tailored to project specifications.
  • Maintain and update backend components to support ongoing project requirements.

- Integration

  • Develop APIs and middleware for seamless Azure-based system integrations.
  • Coordinate integration workflows across multiple platforms and services.

- Security & Compliance

  • Implement controls to meet public sector security and compliance standards.
  • Conduct reviews to ensure all backend services adhere to governance policies.
  • Performance Optimisation
  • Monitor system performance to proactively identify and resolve bottlenecks.
  • Optimise backend processes to consistently achieve project service level agreements.

- Team Collaboration

  • Partner with frontend developers and architects to deliver cohesive solutions.
  • Work alongside DevOps engineers to streamline deployment and integration processes.
  • Project Delivery
  • Contribute to sprint planning and stand-ups to maintain alignment with project objectives.
  • Participate in regular project reviews to monitor progress and ensure timely delivery.

- Required Skills & Experience

Mandatory:

  • Proven experience in backend development using .NET Core and Python.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field.
  • Hands-on experience with Azure services (Function and Container Apps, App Services).
  • Demonstrated ability to deliver as part of a project team using Agile methodologies.
  • Strong understanding of RESTful APIs and microservices architecture.

Desirable:

  • Microsoft Azure certifications (e.g., AZ-204, AZ-900).
  • Exposure to case management systems and business process applications.
  • Experience in public sector projects with safety, security and justice focus.

Key Performance Indicators:

  • Utilisation target of 80% for direct roles
  • KPI’s achievable for the role that follow SMART guidelines
  • Complete assigned tasks/projects within agreed deadlines.
  • Achieve less than 2% error rate in deliverables.
  • Ensure compliance with company standards and policies.
  • Complete mandatory training within set timelines.
  • Submit reports and documentation accurately and on time.


Please send your CV or have conversation directly to Julie Nguyen

Email: Julie.nguyen@seers.net.au

Phone Number: 0425 858 991.

- Min 4+ relevant exp in Application support
- Troubleshooting, Debugging Java and JavaScript

content.unEditedContent:
Hiring !! - Application Support Engineer - AI

  • 12 months contract opportunity- with a high potential to go perm
  • Location: Sydney CBD
  • Hybrid work arrangements - 2 days from office and 3 days WFH
  • Offering $470/day including super

As an Application/Technical Support Engineer, you will be responsible to resolve technical cases created by customers looking for help to understand or troubleshoot unexpected behaviors or to answer technical questions about the software and platform.

Responsibilities:

  • Customer Advocate providing support to users/administrators of our platform. Contributing to the growth of best practices for delivery of support services.
  • Understanding of platform, cloud technologies and troubleshooting practices to ensure successful resolution of challenging technical situations.
  • Providing input across business units regarding process and product improvements when working on technical issues for customers.
  • Resolve issues through various technologies including web, chat, email, case updates, as well as direct telephone support.
  • Using AI-powered tools, automating workflows, analyzing AI-driven insights.

Skills required:

  • Min 4+ years of related experience within either application technical support or software development environment.
  • Demonstrated creative problem-solving approach and strong analytical skills
  • Ability to troubleshoot difficult technical issues with ease and complexity.
  • Ability to read and troubleshoot basic Java and JavaScript code.
  • Proficient in analyzing log files and standard debugging concepts.
  • Advanced MySQL / Postgres knowledge and hands on experience (Oracle also helpful but optional).
  • Prior experience leveraging AI in an application support or software development role
  • Some understanding of remote administration via PowerShell or any other.
  • Excellent communication skills both written and verbal.

Please Note: Due to the nature of the role, Candidates Must be based in Sydney and must be Permanent Residents or Citizens to be considered for this role.

To apply, click the link and upload your current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx), or please send through a copy of your latest CV to rsharma@welovesalt.com for a confidential discussion.

content.jobHook: We’re seeking an experienced Front-End Developer for a 6-month Sydney contract, specialising in React and React Native across web and mobile.
content.unEditedContent:
Overview

We are looking for a Front-End Developer (React / React Native) for a 6 Month Contract based in North Sydney (2 days a week in the office). We’re looking for a highly skilled Front-End Developer with deep experience across React and React Native to join a major design system uplift and component modernization program. This is a 6-month contract based in Sydney, working closely with UX, Product, and Engineering teams to improve consistency, performance, and scalability across web and mobile platforms. The client is a leader in their field and looking for someone to help define their mobile space. 

What You’ll Be Doing Platform & Design System Work
  • Audit and analyse existing React/React Native components.
  • Identify duplication, anti-patterns, styling drift, and coupling issues.
  • Benchmark performance (Lighthouse, Web Vitals, React Profiler, RN perf tools) and accessibility (axe).
  • Ensure full parity between Figma and code (tokens, variants, states).
  • Participate in design–dev reviews and provide technical feasibility insights.
  • Clarify component behaviour, props, interactions, and edge cases.
Refactor & Build
  • Refactor components into modular, composable, typed structures (TypeScript).
  • Implement/strengthen design tokens and theming (light/dark modes, brand variants).
  • Build comprehensive Storybook stories that reflect all Figma states/variants.
  • Replace legacy styling with modern CSS-in-JS or utility-first patterns.
  • Ensure visual and behavioural consistency across web and React Native.
Testing & Quality
  • Add unit/integration tests using Jest, React Testing Library, Cypress.
  • Use Storybook for visual regression and design reviews.
  • Integrate linting, formatting, and type checks into CI (ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript).
Accessibility & Internationalisation
  • Ensure ARIA correctness, keyboard navigation, semantic structure.
  • Validate screen reader and gesture support on mobile.
  • Ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all components.
Documentation & Enablement
  • Document components and patterns in Storybook/Docs.
  • Create contribution guidelines, coding standards, and migration notes.
  • Partner closely with UX/Product on specs and acceptance criteria.
What You Bring
  • 5+ years experience building UI with React and React Native (iOS + Android). You must have experience with React Native!
  • Strong proficiency with React 18+, TypeScript, ES6+.
  • Skilled with modern component architecture, hooks, context, and state management (Redux, Zustand, Recoil).
  • Advanced CSS expertise: Flexbox, Grid, custom properties, selectors.
  • Experience with Styled-JSX (highly advantageous).
  • Confident configuring Webpack (via react-app-rewired), Babel, Sass/CSS preprocessors.
  • Expert-level SASS (mixins, variables, modular architecture).
  • Strong design system experience — tokens, component specs, Figma collaboration.
  • Storybook (web) and Expo/Storybook (RN).
  • Testing experience with Jest, React Testing Library, Detox.
  • Accessibility best practices (WCAG 2.1 AA).
  • Performance profiling/optimisation for both web and RN.
  • Comfortable with CI/CD (Bitbucket/GitHub/GitLab), branching strategies, code reviews.
  • Strong communication and documentation skills — able to clearly explain complex UI concepts.
  • Comfortable collaborating in Figma, Miro, Confluence.
If interested please apply or email chouston@siriustechnology.com.au

title: Senior .NET Developer

content.jobHook: Join a major government organisation to lead the design, development, and support of enterprise applications that drive digital capability.
content.unEditedContent:
Senior .NET Developer

10 Month Initial Contract
Day Rate: $800-$905 plus Super
Start Date: ASAP /Immediate Preferred
Location: Sydney CBD (Hybrid 3 days in office)

Join a major government organisation to lead the design, development, and support of enterprise applications that drive digital capability and innovation.

About the Role
As the Senior .NET Developer, you will be responsible for maintaining and continuously improving enterprise application platforms, ensuring they remain current, efficient, and high-performing. You will lead development activities, deliver system enhancements, and ensure solutions meet scope, budget, schedule, and quality expectations.

Required Skills & Experience
  • 10+ years of hands-on .NET development experience (full stack).
  • Advanced expertise in enterprise application development within large corporate environments.
  • Strong experience with modern JavaScript frameworks (React, Angular or Vue)
  • Proven ability to build and maintain enterprise web applications.
  • Experience managing development and production environments.
  • Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and modern DevOps practices.
  • Exposure to complex, enterprise-scale IT environments.
  • Solid technical project management capability.
  • Strong experience with MS SQL, including SSRS/SSIS.
  • Excellent analytical, documentation, and written communication skills.
Desirable Skills
  • SharePoint development
  • Dynamics CRM development
  • BizTalk development
If you are interested click "apply" or email me a copy of your CV to oliver.iles-mann@ashdownpeople.com.au

content.jobHook: Join a high-performance engineering organisation developing advanced sensor and embedded platforms
content.unEditedContent:
A leading defence technology company is seeking a Systems Integration Software Engineer to design, build, and maintain the internal software and tools that connect embedded systems, test environments, and production workflows. This role sits at the intersection of embedded engineering, backend development, and system integration, supporting both rapid prototyping and high-reliability production lines.
  
Working closely with engineers across multiple disciplines, you’ll deliver the tooling and platform components that ensure consistent, robust operation across diverse devices and environments. You’ll help evolve legacy systems, introduce new automation capabilities, and drive improvements in reliability, documentation, and developer experience.
  
Key Responsibilities
  • Develop and maintain software services, tools, and interfaces for embedded Linux platforms.
  • Implement new features and internal applications supporting R&D and production workflows.
  • Collaborate with hardware, firmware, and software teams to ensure seamless system-level integration.
  • Create and maintain high-quality documentation, diagrams, and internal guides.
  • Support and modernise older tools and applications, improving maintainability and performance.
  • Provide technical expertise, mentoring, and code review to colleagues.  
Skills & Experience
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Mechatronics, Electronic Engineering, or equivalent experience.
  • 5–7+ years’ experience in software or systems engineering roles.
  • Strong command-line and development experience in Linux environments.
  • Proficiency in Python (required) and experience with Golang or modern C++.
  • Familiarity with embedded Linux development workflows.
  • Competence with DevOps and development tooling (Git, Jira, Confluence, Docker, GitLab CI/CD, Poetry).
  • Experience with networked communication protocols such as gRPC, REST, TCP/UDP, or other high-performance messaging approaches.
  • Background in developing tools or systems used in manufacturing, validation, or production environments.
  • Strong unit testing experience and comfort working with CI/CD pipelines.
Desirable
  • Exposure to automated testing frameworks, DevOps workflows, and system monitoring.
  • Experience integrating with lab instrumentation via VISA or similar interfaces.
  • Knowledge of RF test equipment or hardware validation workflows.
  • Familiarity with microservice architectures, API design, and cross-cutting concerns (logging, persistence, configuration, etc.).  
Personal Attributes
  • Exceptional written communication and documentation skills.
  • Strong debugging and troubleshooting instincts.
  • Close attention to detail and a disciplined approach to engineering practice.
  • Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to a fast-moving team.
  • Comfortable managing multiple threads of work under time pressure.
  • Proactive, supportive, and willing to assist across diverse engineering tasks when needed.
You can contact me at farid@thedrivegroup.com.au or 0435777965 for more info

content.jobHook: Join a leading design product business in their discovery team who are responsible for advancing the company’s search and analytics capability.
content.unEditedContent:
“Everyone knows what search engines are. But relatively few know how to use them effectively.” – Marc Ostrofsky

As Senior Software Engineer in the Search and Discovery Team, you will be tasked with optimising the search and navigation across of one of Australia’s most sort after design businesses.

About the Company This Sydney-based global design company (backed by Airtree) that empowers small businesses to build their brand instantly. With a loyal customer base in Australia and the US, they are now focused on making their platform the best in the world.

The company has just rebranded their small-business branding application, anticipating more traffic and engagement. They are already leveraging AI, and have a dedicated AI team to develop image-based generative AI, keeping them ahead of the competition.

Their office is in Surry Hills, but they value flexibility. Most of the team comes in one day a fortnight, and they have monthly team events. If you prefer the office, there's a core group that goes in regularly, and they even have a coffee tab next door!

About the Role This company is looking for a Senior Engineer to join their Agile, quality-focused team. You'll drive developing and improving a large customer-facing platform, handling tasks from database design to integration development.

Experience with Elasticsearch is essential to optimise search functionality, ensuring fast, scalable, and accurate results for users. Prior data analytics experience will also be crucial for improving the platform's search capabilities, allowing deeper insights into customer behaviors and enhancing overall search performance to deliver a superior user experience.

You'll build and deploy new functionalities, support existing features, enhance site scalability, infrastructure, and testing, all in a highly collaborative environment.

They have weekly highlight and feature meetings, perfect for showcasing your work and staying updated on product developments.


Got the tech?
  • Elasticsearch or Opensearch (Bonus if you have this but a keen interest if not is a must)
  • .Net Core
  • Cloud
  • CI/CD
  • Event Driven Architecture as it relates to Microservices
  • Any Javascript framework (Vue, React, Angular)
Benefits
  • Flexible working arrangements (1 Day Office per fortnight)
  • Work with a team of innovators and tech enthusiasts (AI already introduced)
  • Equity in the company
  • Career progression in a fast-growing company
  • Talented team and fun culture with quarterly events and team lunches
If you're ready to make an impact and grow with a dynamic and supportive team, I would love to hear from you! Apply directly now to join their journey.

Dani - danielle@theonset.com.au or Karl - Karl@theonset.com.au

content.jobHook: Lead AI adoption and build secure, scalable solutions. Hands-on role with AWS Bedrock and Python/Go experience required.
content.unEditedContent:
TRG are partnering with a fast-growing technology organisation working at the intersection of AI, automation, and secure systems. This is a unique opportunity to join a newly formed team focused on enabling AI adoption across the business and driving innovation at scale.

About the Role

As the first member of the AI Enablement team, you'll design and deliver intelligent tools and workflows that improve efficiency and unlock new capabilities. You'll collaborate with IT and Security teams to ensure solutions are robust, compliant, and seamlessly integrated into existing systems.

This is a hands-on engineering role with a strategic edge, ideal for someone who enjoys building solutions from the ground up while shaping long-term AI strategy.

Key Responsibilities

  • Identify and prioritise opportunities for AI-driven automation across business units
  • Design and implement secure, scalable internal tools and integrations
  • Develop APIs and workflows to connect systems and streamline processes
  • Partner with stakeholders to validate requirements and deliver practical solutions
  • Ensure compliance with security and governance standards
  • Track and report key performance metrics to leadership

Essential Skills & Experience

  • 5+ years in software engineering or automation roles
  • Strong programming experience in Python, Go, or similar languages
  • Solid understanding of cloud platforms, containerisation, and infrastructure automation
  • Experience building APIs and integrating systems
  • Hands-on experience with AWS Bedrock for deploying AI-powered applications
  • Excellent problem-solving and communication skills

Desirable Skills

  • Exposure to AI/ML tools and frameworks
  • Familiarity with modern automation platforms and orchestration tools
  • Knowledge of secure application design and best practices
  • Experience with hybrid or multi-cloud environments
  • Ability to think strategically and deliver solutions that scale

Why This Role?

  • Shape the future of AI adoption in a high-growth tech organisation
  • Work with the latest technologies in a secure, mission-critical environment
  • Join a collaborative team with strong career development opportunities

content.jobHook: Driving innovation with cutting-edge technology for a secure, connected future
content.unEditedContent:
We’re on the lookout for a number of Embedded Systems Engineers to join a product development team in Sydney. Reporting to the Systems Engineering Manager, these roles offer the opportunity to make a direct impact in a niche, fast-growing defence technology sector.
  
You’ll be at the heart of developing current and next-generation products, bridging the gap between stakeholder needs and engineering outcomes. This means gathering, analysing, and managing requirements, ensuring complex systems come together seamlessly, and empowering development teams to work towards common goals. If you thrive on autonomy, responsibility, and collaboration, this role is for you.
  
What you’ll do
  • Work with a team of systems engineers across sensors & effectors and product development to define and deliver new capabilities.
  • Develop and embed systems engineering processes that promote best practice, efficiency, and quality.
  • Manage documentation of system design, requirements, and test strategies, with a strong focus on integration testing.
  • Act as the bridge between stakeholders and engineering teams, communicating risks, issues, and progress.
  • Own technical problems, coordinate cross-functional solutions, and drive continual product improvement.
  • Partner with Product and Project Management to align deliverables and milestones.
  • Bring the customer’s voice into development teams, ensuring new features meet real-world needs.
What you’ll bring
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s in Systems, Mechatronics, Electrical, Mechanical, Computer Science, or similar.
  • 3+ years’ experience in systems engineering within complex product development.
  • Strong grasp of systems engineering frameworks, tools, and lifecycle principles.
  • Experience with requirements management for hardware/software systems.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively, taking ownership of deliverables.
  • Desirable: experience with AI-enabled systems, RF hardware/software, electronic warfare, or distributed systems.
  • Bonus: exposure to cybersecurity in systems engineering, Atlassian tools (Jira/Confluence).
  • A drive to excel in a high-empowerment, fast-paced, mission-driven environment.
Bonus Points For
  • Exposure to electronic warfare, artificial intelligence, or machine learning domains.
  • Experience conducting product demonstrations, gathering end-user feedback, or supporting external product events.
This is a fantastic opportunity for systems-minded engineers ready to take on a diverse, high-impact role within a company at the forefront of counter-threat and autonomous system technologies.

For more info you can contact me at farid@thedrivegroup.com.au or click Apply and send through your details and I'll be in touch!

title: Senior Guidewire Developer

content.jobHook:
> Work anywhere from Australia
>Leading insurance client
>Longtime opportunity
content.unEditedContent:
Adactin Group is a 100% Australian-owned global IT consulting company, headquartered in Sydney. We are a trusted technology partner helping organizations drive end-to-end digital transformation through our deep expertise in Microsoft and AWS ecosystems, AI-powered Digital Engineering, and future-ready Quality Assurance.

Our managed delivery approach, backed by domain expertise, ensures successful outcomes for every project. At Adactin, we believe in the power of our people. We foster a dynamic and supportive environment where your career can grow alongside exciting projects that shape the future of technology. Join us, and be part of a team committed to innovation, excellence, and making a difference.


Role Description


We are seeking Senior Guidewire Developers(Claims) for a full-time remote role, In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining Guidewire applications especially in claims domain, implementing system configurations, and integrating third-party systems. Day-to-day tasks include programming, troubleshooting, testing, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality solutions aligned with business requirements. This role requires a commitment to adherence to best practices in software development and contributing to process improvements


Only Candidates based out of Australia will be considered

Job Description

  • Experience in Guidewire ClaimCenter (CC) development, including configuration and integration.

  • Minimum 3-5 years of Guidewire ClaimCenter development experience is required.

  • Strong understanding of the full SDLC with hands-on experience in Agile/Iterative development commonly used in Guidewire implementations.

  • Expertise in configuring and developing key areas of ClaimCenter, including FNOL, Claim Transactions, Exposures, and related claim workflows.

  • Participate in business and functional requirement discussions to translate requirements into technical specifications for ClaimCenter enhancements.

  • Review design documents and ensure high-quality implementation aligned with best practices.

  • Design, develop, and unit test ClaimCenter configurations and customizations using GOSU and Guidewire best practices.

  • Minimum 2-3 years of GOSU programming experience is required.

  • Develop and implement integration plugins to support data exchange between ClaimCenter, internal systems, and external vendors.

  • Perform defect triage and apply fixes across System Testing, End-to-End Testing, and UAT phases.

  • Maintain code quality, enforce coding standards, and ensure overall solution stability.

  • Propose scalable technical solutions for new features and enhancements.

  • Design and develop new ClaimCenter components, rules, and workflows.

  • Implement custom frameworks for validation rules, assignment rules, and claim automation logic.

  • Experience working with SQL Server, Oracle, and/or DB2 databases


For more information, Email me at aswini.abhishek@adactin.com

content.jobHook: Design cutting-edge algorithms that shape the future of RF signal intelligence
content.unEditedContent:
A new opportunity is available for a Senior Signal Processing Engineer to join a dynamic development team working on high-impact RF signal intelligence systems. This role focuses on algorithm development within a rapidly growing niche sector, contributing to next-generation capabilities in real-time signal detection, classification, and threat identification.
  
The role will focus on designing, optimising, and deploying advanced signal processing techniques into embedded platforms, working with technologies such as Software Defined Radios (SDR), FPGAs, DSPs, and GPUs. It involves deep collaboration with engineers across embedded systems, software, and product development teams.

Key Responsibilities
  • Research and develop cutting-edge signal processing methods to detect and characterise signals in the RF spectrum
  • Design and implement algorithms for embedded deployment on SDR, FPGA, DSP, and GPU platforms
  • Build simulation tools to support the training, testing, and validation of new algorithmic models
  • Analyse complex time-series, frequency, and spatial datasets to extract actionable intelligence
  • Provide technical leadership in the areas of algorithm design, system modelling, and mathematical formulation
  • Integrate signal processing models into scalable, real-world production systems
  • Optimise algorithms for performance, efficiency, and hardware constraints
  • Document code and maintain a structured repository to support ongoing development and versioning
Skills and Experience Required
  • Degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field
  • Minimum 5 years’ experience in algorithm design, DSP, or signal intelligence
  • Strong experience in real-time signal processing, RF analysis, or Electronic Warfare applications
  • Proficiency in building high-performance algorithms that process sensor and RF data
  • Practical knowledge of time-series analysis, spectral decomposition, sensor fusion, or statistical modelling
  • Ability to collaborate within multidisciplinary teams and communicate technical concepts clearly
  • Experience working in Linux environments and using modern development tools
Desirable Attributes
  • Familiarity with SDR platforms and spectrum analysis techniques
  • Experience with simulation frameworks and modelling environments
  • Exposure to RF communications, radar, or EW-related systems
  • Self-motivated, curious, and passionate about applying complex theory to real-world challenges
  • Comfortable mentoring team members and advocating for algorithmic best practices
  • A mindset geared toward continuous learning, experimentation, and technical excellence
This role is ideal for someone who thrives in an R&D-rich environment and wants to play a direct role in shaping signal processing capabilities used in real-time defence applications.

If this role sounds good for you, please click Apply and send through your details or you can email me at farid@thedrivegroup.com.au for more details

content.jobHook: Drive innovation in sensor technologies through high-performance software
content.unEditedContent:
We’re looking for a talented and driven Embedded Software Engineer to help build, scale, and support complex sensor systems operating in real-world environments. In this role, you’ll design and implement embedded software that runs on Linux-based systems, contributing to projects that push the boundaries of real-time data processing, RF signal detection, and automated response.
  
You’ll work closely with teams spanning AI/ML, data science, hardware engineering, and cloud infrastructure to create robust solutions capable of handling mission-critical workloads. The software you build will directly support applications in security, defence, and remote surveillance.

What You’ll Be Doing
  • Develop and maintain embedded software features on Linux-based edge systems
  • Collaborate across disciplines to meet operational and performance requirements
  • Ensure code is designed for resource-constrained, high-uptime environments
  • Implement features using modern C++ and Python, depending on project needs
  • Participate in software design discussions, code reviews, and performance tuning
  • Work on inter-process communication mechanisms for modular, scalable architectures
  • Create and maintain high-quality technical documentation for implemented components
  • Contribute to the continuous integration pipeline and system testing frameworks
  • Support system monitoring, logging, and observability in production deployments
  • Engage with DevOps and infrastructure specialists to improve deployment workflows
Your Background
  • Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field
  • Proven experience developing applications in modern C++ 
  • Strong understanding of Linux development (embedded Linux preferred)
  • Familiarity with multi-process systems and IPC mechanisms
  • Exposure to Docker, Git, CI/CD pipelines (e.g., GitLab), and versioning tools
  • Experience with design patterns, testing, and scalable software architecture
Bonus Points For
  • Knowledge of concurrency, performance optimisation, or low-latency systems
  • Hands-on experience with Go programming
  • Background in machine learning, RF signal processing, or DSP
  • Exposure to FPGA, sensor integration, or systems running in remote deployments
  • Understanding of DevOps practices, including observability and automated deployments
Interested candidates should click Apply and send through their details, or you can email me at farid@thedrivegroup.com.au for more info or call me on 0435777965!

bulletPoints: Lead the evolution of a major enterprise web platform
Vibrant campus life based in Macquarie Park
Discounts on products and services
content.jobHook: Work with engineering, UX, content, and analytics teams to drive platform strategy, technical excellence, and customer-centric outcomes
content.unEditedContent:
Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

We're seeking a Technical Product Manager to lead the evolution of a major enterprise web platform. This role is critical in delivering seamless, secure, and high-performing digital experiences to customers. You'll work closely with engineering, UX, content, and analytics teams to drive platform strategy, technical excellence, and customer-centric outcomes.

The day to day

  • Own the technical product strategy and roadmap for a large-scale web experience platform.
  • Ensure platform readiness, performance, and reliability through proactive monitoring and incident management.
  • Lead delivery of high-quality web features and content with strong SEO and accessibility compliance.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance customer experience and engagement.
  • Drive continuous improvement by addressing technical debt and improving scalability and reusability.
  • Manage stakeholder relationships and align platform evolution with business goals.
  • Champion a culture of innovation, ownership, and strategic thinking within the team.
  • Support both front-end and back-end development initiatives with a strong engineering mindset.
  • Embed quality assurance practices throughout the development lifecycle to ensure robust testing, code quality, and defect prevention.

What makes you a great fit?

  • Technical Product Management experience across platform, web application development, devops, cloud, SRE, or security domains.
  • Strong front-end engineering background with exposure to back-end technologies.
  • Experience with Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) or similar enterprise CMS platforms.
  • Solid understanding of API integration, web architecture, and SaaS environments.
  • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
  • Strategic thinker with a clear vision, ownership mentality, and ability to bring ideas to life.
  • High attention to detail and strong sense of customer/user experience.
  • Familiarity with accessibility standards and compliance.
    Nice to Have:
  • Experience with Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
  • Prior experience as an Engineering Manager or Tech Lead.
  • Exposure to FinOps, compliance automation, or large-scale platform migrations.

The good stuff….

  • Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
  • Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
  • Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.
  • Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
  • We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
  • Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
  • Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
  • Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity

content.jobHook: Work for a leading AI-led start-up as they scale their Data function. Multiple opportunities to make a difference with solid career pathways.
content.unEditedContent:
We’re working exclusively with a start-up building data-driven products that process large volumes of structured data from multiple external systems. We’re looking for multiple Data Engineers to help build, run, and improve the data pipelines that power their core platform. 

This is a hands-on role focused on data ingestion, transformation, integrations, and issue resolution. The role suits junior-mid level data engineers who enjoy practical problem-solving and learning by doing. 

Location: Sydney (1 day in office/week)
Salary: $120k Base + Super + Equity

What You’ll Do:
  • Build and maintain data pipelines ingesting data from many external systems
  • Develop and support integrations with third-party platforms
  • Investigate and resolve data quality and pipeline issues
  • Work with engineers and analysts to improve data reliability and usability
  • Support onboarding by understanding source systems and data structures
  • Improve internal tooling, processes, and documentation
  • Help scale systems as data volume and complexity grow
What You’ll Bring:
  • Solid SQL skills and comfort working with structured data
  • Experience with Python, or the ability to learn it quickly
  • Understanding of databases and data transformation concepts
  • Exposure to data pipelines, ETL, or backend scripting (through work or study)
  • Clear communication and collaborative approach
  • Relevant degree or equivalent practical experience
  • Version control and basic DevOps practices
  • Experience in a fast-moving engineering environment
If you have any questions about this role or others we have available you can email Jack directly via jack@thedrivegroup.com.au
  
For The Latest Jobs, Tech News or to 'Introduce A Friend' for a $1000 Referral Fee - Follow Us On Linkedin:
  
https://www.linkedin.com/company/thedrivegroup
  
TheDriveGroup is 100% committed to improving meaningful diversity in the technology industry.
  
We partner with clients who embrace diversity and seek candidates across all backgrounds, abilities, genders, sexualities, cultures, and faiths.
  
If you have the right skills and attitude we would love to hear from you!

title: Web Designer

bulletPoints: Part-time opportunity with clear pathway to full-time
Design-led Web Designer role
Hands-on agency environment
content.unEditedContent:
About the Company

Lula Group Web Design EST 2020 is a fast-growing, Shopify-focused web design and development agency based in Liverpool, Sydney. We partner with eCommerce and service-based businesses to design and build high-performing, conversion-focused websites across Shopify, WordPress, and custom platforms.

We’re known for our hands-on approach, clean design, practical UX decisions, and strong client communication. With multiple projects already confirmed and steady inbound work, we’re entering an exciting growth phase.

Lula Group operates under a group structure, with specialist arms across web design and recruitment. While this role sits entirely within the web team, being part of the broader group means exposure to a wide range of industries, founders, and growing businesses across Sydney.

About the Role

This is a part-time, hands-on Web Designer role focused on designing clean, user-friendly, conversion-driven websites with a working understanding of how those designs are built.

You’ll lead the visual and UX side of projects while collaborating closely with developers to ensure designs are practical, build-ready, and scalable. While this is not a developer role, you’ll be comfortable making small layout or styling updates directly within Shopify, WordPress, or Wix when required.

This role suits a designer who enjoys owning their work, thinking strategically about user journeys, and designing with real-world build constraints in mind.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design website layouts and page structures for Shopify, WordPress, and service-based websites
  • Create wireframes, page flows, and visual concepts with UX and CRO in mind
  • Design responsive layouts with a strong mobile-first approach
  • Collaborate with developers to ensure designs are technically feasible and build-ready
  • Make light content, layout, or styling updates inside CMS platforms where required
  • Apply basic understanding of HTML/CSS to support cleaner handover and implementation
  • Support website refreshes, redesigns, and ongoing client updates
  • Participate in client calls to understand goals, feedback, and design direction
  • Work closely with the Founder on creative direction, scopes, and timelines

Skills & Experience

  • Proven experience as a Web Designer, Digital Designer, or UX/UI Designer
  • Strong portfolio showcasing website design work (Shopify or eCommerce experience preferred)
  • Solid understanding of UX, user journeys, hierarchy, and conversion principles
  • Working knowledge of Shopify, WordPress, or Wix environments
  • Basic understanding of HTML/CSS (enough to design with build constraints in mind)
  • Experience designing for eCommerce or service-based businesses
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines
  • Strong communication skills and attention to detail

What’s in It for You

  • Part-time role with 2 days per week in-office (Liverpool)
  • Clear pathway to full-time as the agency grows
  • Design-led role with real input into project outcomes
  • Close collaboration with the Founder and development team
  • Exposure to a wide range of brands, industries, and website types
  • Supportive, founder-led environment with autonomy and ownership
  • Opportunity to grow into a senior designer or creative lead role

How to Apply

To apply, please click ‘Apply’ and submit your resume and portfolio.

For a confidential discussion about the role, contact Jovana at hiring@lulagroup.com.au.

Please note: Applications will only be accepted from candidates currently based in Sydney, NSW, or those with immediate relocation plans.
